cognitive_layer.purpose: CellTypist uses machine learning models trained on reference data to automatically predict cell types from gene expression patterns. It provides both predicted labels and confidence scores, enabling rapid annotation of cell populations without manual marker selection.

parameter_impact.model: Different models are trained on different tissue/cell type references. 'Immune_All_Low.pkl' covers many immune cell types. Other models exist for specific tissues.
parameter_impact.majority_voting: When True, performs over-clustering then votes within each cluster for the final label. Provides more robust annotations for rare populations.
parameter_impact.p_thres: Probability threshold for multi-label classification mode. Higher values require stronger evidence for prediction.

error_handling.ValueError: Genes not found in model: Ensure gene symbols match the model's expected format. CellTypist requires gene symbols, not IDs.
error_handling.KeyError: predicted_labels: Annotation failed. Check if input data is properly normalized and contains sufficient genes.

wrong_tissue.adjust: use model trained on relevant tissue (e.g., Tissue_All.pkl for broad tissue coverage)
wrong_tissue.causal_chain: Different models are trained on different reference datasets. Wrong tissue model will have poor accuracy due to missing or mismatched cell types.
wrong_tissue.expected_effect: Better alignment with expected cell populations

noisy_predictions.causal_chain: Over-clustering followed by voting within clusters smooths out noise in individual cell predictions, providing more robust annotations.
noisy_predictions.expected_effect: More consistent cell type assignment within clusters
